Title: RETICULATA1 is a plastid-localized basic amino acid transporter

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: DUF3411-containing RETICULATA family membrane protein; novel plastid-containing-lineage protein family; conserved C-terminal GxQ motif
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Not restricted to a single organ or developmental stage; mainly expressed in vasculature and surrounding cells
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: 14-day-old seedlings and leaves analyzed; expression reduced upon exogenous Arg, Citr, His, Lys or Orn feeding
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: loss-of-function mutant analysis (re-6); rescue by RE1 overexpression; heterologous yeast complementation; proteoliposome transport assay; chloroplast metabolite profiling; 15N-ammonium labeling
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes plastid/basic amino acid homeostasis; loss of RE1 reduces Arg, Lys, Orn and Citr levels, impairs de novo basic amino acid biosynthesis, causes reticulate leaves and hypersensitivity to exogenous basic amino acids
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RE1 encodes a plastid inner-envelope basic amino acid transporter. 2. re-6 loss-of-function mutants show reduced basic amino acid content in seedlings and chloroplasts and altered amino acid homeostasis. 3. RE1 transports Orn, Arg, Citr and Lys in vitro and is required for normal leaf development and nutrient allocation.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Double knockout with RER1 is lethal, indicating functional overlap and essentiality. Overexpression in re-6 rescues phenotype and can elevate several amino acids including Citr, His, Lys, Orn, Phe and Trp.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Exogenous Lys, Arg, Citr, Orn, His or Met feeding; 15N-ammonium labeling
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss causes reticulate leaf phenotype, growth impairment, reduced seed production when RER1 dosage is reduced, and amino acid imbalance
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to enhance essential/basic amino acid content in plants through overexpression or pathway engineering, though whole-plant metabolic effects and developmental tradeoffs require evaluation
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Essential amino acids for plant metabolism and human nutrition; support development and seed filling
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Proteomics of isolated chloroplasts; co-expression analysis; no public accession reported
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: UBIQUITIN10 promoter for overexpression constructs; endogenous promoter in native analyses

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: DUF3411-containing RETICULATA-RELATED membrane protein; conserved C-terminal GxQ motif
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Intracellular transport and homeostasis of basic amino acids across the plastid envelope
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Not restricted to a single organ or developmental stage; mainly expressed in vasculature and surrounding cells
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: mRNA generally reduced upon exogenous basic amino acid feeding
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR-Cas9 mutant generation in re-6 background; genetic interaction analysis; complementation of re-6 by overexpression; yeast complementation; subcellular localization
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts redundantly with RE1 in plastid basic amino acid transport; alone shows weak phenotype but reduced dosage in re-6 background aggravates developmental and fertility defects
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Functional overlap/redundant control of plastid basic amino acid transport capacity
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Exogenous basic amino acids lower transcript abundance; Lys and Citr are proposed to inhibit transport activity
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Proposed involvement in feedback-related amino acid homeostasis because Lys/Citr treatment likely inhibits transport, contributing to feedback inhibition of Arg/Orn biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent compensatory controller of plastid basic amino acid homeostasis together with RE1
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RER1 is the closest homolog of RE1 and localizes to the plastid inner envelope. 2. RER1 complements yeast arg11 similarly to RE1, supporting a similar substrate function. 3. Loss of both RE1 and RER1 is lethal, showing that RER1 provides essential overlapping activity in basic amino acid transport/homeostasis.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Single rer1 mutants lacked major metabolic phenotype under tested conditions, so its in planta function is inferred mainly from redundancy and yeast assays rather than direct transport assays in liposomes.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Exogenous Arg, Citr, Lys, Orn feeding; CRISPR knockout in re-6 background
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Combined deficiency with RE1 is lethal; reduced RER1 dosage in re-6 shortens siliques and reduces seed set
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Possible complementary engineering target with RE1 for improving basic amino acid allocation, but redundancy and lethality caution against strong loss-of-function manipulation
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Putative homohexameric complex proposed for DUF3411 proteins, not fully resolved experimentally
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Basic amino acids contribute to plant development, nitrogen allocation, storage protein synthesis and human nutrition
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Co-expression/expression analyses; no public accession reported
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: U6-26 promoter for gRNAs; EC1.2 promoter for Cas9; At2S3 promoter for seed GFP marker; UBIQUITIN10 promoter for overexpression
